title: Tool Use Gate Evidence
purpose: Proves the executable tool-use security gate subset, links to [`security-redaction.md`](../security-redaction.md), and separates implemented branches from reserved policy vocabulary.

implemented_actions.name: allow
implemented_actions.summary: explicit policy bundle allow proceeds without provider block

implemented_actions.name: require-approval
implemented_actions.summary: classified risk without bundle allow may continue only through a human approval branch

implemented_actions.name: interrupt-and-block
implemented_actions.summary: unknown tier/surface or unavailable approval path blocks provider tool use

reserved_actions.name: allow-but-audit
reserved_actions.summary: reserved branch only; no executable ToolUseAction exists yet

reserved_actions.name: quarantine
reserved_actions.summary: reserved branch only; no executable ToolUseAction exists yet

facts.name: policy owns stable risk surfaces
facts.summary: network egress, protected path write, secret exposure, and destructive command are typed ToolUseSurface values.

facts.name: policy decision is pure
facts.summary: EvaluateToolUse returns C7 decisions without provider execution.

facts.name: toolpolicy maps provider-neutral tools to surfaces
facts.summary: ClassifyToolUseSurface derives risk surfaces from kind, name, and redacted args.

facts.name: redacted args become secret exposure signals
facts.summary: toolargs redacts sensitive keys/patterns and toolpolicy treats redaction markers as secret exposure.

facts.name: supervisor stores redacted provider tool payloads
facts.summary: provider tool events preserve bounded ToolRef.Args without rehydrating raw secrets.

facts.name: headless tool approval is fail-closed
facts.summary: approval/start gates block classified risk when policy lacks an allow path.

absent_surfaces.name: reserved actions are not implemented in policy
absent_surfaces.reason: reserved vocabulary must not be claimed as executable without new branch evidence.

absent_surfaces.name: toolpolicy does not execute provider processes
absent_surfaces.reason: toolpolicy classifies and gates; session/runtime own execution and cancellation.

source_checks.name: session-approval-blocks
source_checks.contains: tool approval unavailable in headless run

assertions: implemented tool-use branches are allow, require-approval, and interrupt-and-block only
allow-but-audit and quarantine remain reserved vocabulary until new evidence exists
toolpolicy classifies and gates but does not execute provider processes
ToolRef.Args evidence is bounded and redacted before IR projection
headless classified tool use fails closed when no policy allow path exists
